task_completion.description: Open the three raw TIFF images in ImageJ.
task_completion.visual_evidence: ImageJ/Fiji interface shows mitochondria_raw.tif, cytoskeleton_raw.tif, and nucleus_raw.tif open in separate image windows.

task_completion.description: Check the microscope_metadata.txt file for spatial calibration information.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal window shows the contents of microscope_metadata.txt, revealing the pixel size.

task_completion.description: Merge the three images into a multi-channel composite with correct color assignments.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Merge Channels' dialog shows C1 (Red) = mitochondria_raw.tif, C2 (Green) = cytoskeleton_raw.tif, and C3 (Blue) = nucleus_raw.tif, OR a composite image window is visible displaying the merged result with these colors.

task_completion.description: Apply spatial calibration of 0.16 microns per pixel.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The ImageJ 'Properties' or 'Set Scale' dialog is open with pixel width/height set to 0.16 and unit set to 'micron', or the image window title/status bar reflects the calibrated physical dimensions.

task_completion.description: Adjust the brightness and contrast of the Green channel (cytoskeleton).
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'B&C' (Brightness/Contrast) window is open and active while the Green channel is selected, or the composite image shows a visibly enhanced green cytoskeleton compared to its raw state.

task_completion.description: Save the resulting multi-channel composite TIFF to the correct path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Save As' dialog is open pointing to /home/ga/ImageJ_Data/processed/assembled_cell.tif, or a terminal/file manager confirms the file's existence in the processed directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the ImageJ/Fiji GUI for image processing tasks.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with ImageJ menus (e.g., Image > Color > Merge Channels, Image > Properties, Image > Adjust > Brightness/Contrast) rather than relying solely on terminal image manipulation tools like ImageMagick.

integrity.description: The agent actually opened and read the metadata file.
integrity.visual_evidence: A screenshot shows microscope_metadata.txt open in a text editor or its contents printed in a terminal, proving the agent didn't just guess or use prior knowledge of the 0.16 value.

integrity.description: The agent saved the file from within ImageJ.
integrity.visual_evidence: The ImageJ 'Save As' dialog is visible, or the ImageJ status bar shows saving progress, rather than the file being copied or moved via terminal commands.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The raw images are located in /home/ga/ImageJ_Data/raw/experiment_042. The correct channel mapping is: Channel 1 (Red) = mitochondria_raw.tif, Channel 2 (Green) = cytoskeleton_raw.tif, Channel 3 (Blue) = nucleus_raw.tif. The spatial calibration must be set to 0.16 microns per pixel (tolerance ±0.01) with the unit 'micron'. The final output must be saved as a multi-channel composite TIFF at /home/ga/ImageJ_Data/processed/assembled_cell.tif. The green channel must be visibly adjusted for brightness/contrast.
